Understanding Tax Law Changes for 2025

Published on January 15, 2025 by John Doe, CPA, JD

The 2025 tax year brings significant changes that affect both individuals and businesses. As a board certified tax attorney with 15 years of experience in tax planning, I will walk you through the most important updates and what they mean for your financial strategy.

Key Changes for Individual Taxpayers

The standard deduction has been adjusted for inflation, increasing to $14,600 for single filers and $29,200 for married couples filing jointly. Additionally, several tax brackets have been updated to reflect cost-of-living adjustments.

Business Tax Updates

Small businesses will benefit from expanded Section 179 deductions, allowing immediate expensing of up to $1,220,000 in qualifying equipment purchases. The qualified business income deduction remains in effect with updated thresholds.

What You Should Do Now

We are a licensed and accredited advisory firm. Our certified professionals can help you navigate these changes and optimize your tax strategy for 2025.

About the Author

John Doe is a board certified tax attorney and CPA with 15 years of experience helping clients navigate complex tax situations. He is a member of the American Bar Association and the AICPA.
